scenes.speech: Welcome to the Programming Dojo! Let's make magic with code! 💻
scenes.title: Programming Dojo
scenes.content: Programming is like writing instructions for a computer. We'll start with simple commands and gradually master more complex magic!

scenes.speech: First, let's learn about 'variables'. It's like labeling a box!
scenes.title: Variable Training
scenes.content: A variable is a 'box' to store data. You can put your favorite things into a box with a name.

scenes.speech: Using loops can save you from repeating the same actions!
scenes.title: Loop Training
scenes.content: A loop is used to repeat the same commands multiple times. You can easily write code like 'walk, walk, walk...' three times!

scenes.speech: Conditional branching makes your program smarter!
scenes.title: Conditional Branching Training
scenes.content: Conditional branching is like 'if something, then do this, otherwise do that'. Your program can make choices!

scenes.speech: Let's ask Codex to create a 'Rock, Paper, Scissors' game for us!
scenes.title: Practice: Rock, Paper, Scissors Game
scenes.content: Using AI Codex, let's create a Rock, Paper, Scissors game. What code will it generate?
scenes.highlight:
Challenge:
Ask Codex!
"Create a Rock, Paper, Scissors game in Python. Include explanations"

scenes.title: Organize with Functions
scenes.speech: Functions = box with one name for one job! 📦
scenes.content: When the same logic appears three times, it's time for a function. Name with 'verb + object' so future you can read it.
scenes.highlight:
Sign to wrap:
- Used 3 times
- Name reveals intent

scenes.title: The Debug Way
scenes.content: When you spot a bug, drop print or console.log to check 'I got this far'. Pinpointing the place is half the fix.
scenes.highlight:
Debug 3 steps:
1. Reproduce
2. Add prints
3. Narrow the cause

scenes.speech: Congratulations, you've completed the programming training! Share your work on Slack! 🎉
scenes.title: Training Completed!
scenes.content: You've mastered three essential tools: variables, loops, and conditional branching. Next, challenge yourself to create an original program!
